run.output: Query returned 4 rows, execution time: 14799.43 ms

validation.comparison_result: Correctness: 3 of 4 rows have minor precision errors in floating-point aggregate columns (last decimal place differs—typical rounding error). Performance: 14,799 ms vs. benchmark baseline of 48 ms = 308× SLOWER.

profiling.notes: perf tool not available on system

Q1.notes: Correctness: minor precision loss (rows 0, 2, 3 have rounding differences in sum_disc_price and sum_charge, off by <0.01 ULP). Performance: 308× slower than baseline—critical architectural issue.

Q1.bottleneck: TEXT PARSING BOTTLENECK: Code reads from raw CSV file (lineitem.tbl) and parses each line character-by-character using std::getline + stringstream. Parsing 60M rows with float/decimal conversion is extremely slow.
Q1.root_cause: SEMANTIC ISSUE - Incorrect storage access pattern. Code should use GenDB binary columnar storage API (mmap-based zero-copy reads) but instead uses fallback text parsing. Line 73 hard-codes path to '/home/jl4492/GenDB/benchmarks/tpc-h/data/sf10/lineitem.tbl' instead of loading from the .gendb directory. This causes: (1) 60M-row text parse instead of instant mmap, (2) string tokenization overhead (stringstream is slow), (3) repeated decimal parsing with stod() for every row, (4) exception handling in try-catch loop, (5) no parallel I/O or prefetching.

analysis.overall: CRITICAL ARCHITECTURAL FAILURE. Current Q1 achieves 14,799 ms—308× slower than 48 ms baseline. Root cause: Code uses CSV text parsing instead of GenDB columnar storage. Solution requires: (1) Load binary columnar data from gendb storage directory using mmap or columnar API, (2) Use direct array indexing for aggregation (6 groups only), (3) Apply date filter early, (4) Consider thread-local aggregation for 64-core parallelism. Floating-point precision errors are secondary (acceptable rounding).

critical_fixes.issue: SEMANTIC/ARCH: Code reads from hardcoded CSV file path instead of using GenDB binary columnar storage API. This causes 14,799 ms execution vs. 48 ms baseline.
critical_fixes.fix: Refactor to load from GenDB storage directory. Use mmap-based columnar access for lineitem table. Load only required columns: l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus.
critical_fixes.description:
Lines 73-91 currently parse CSV. Must be replaced with:
1. Load metadata from <gendb_dir>/metadata.json to get column file paths
2. mmap() each binary column file for zero-copy access
3. Use zone maps to skip blocks where l_shipdate > cutoff (pre-computed min/max per block)
4. Process mmap'd arrays directly instead of parsing strings
Estimated speedup: 100-300× (text parsing eliminated). Reference implementation in earlier iterations uses LineitemTable structure with columnar array access.

critical_fixes.issue: PRECISION: Minor floating-point rounding errors in aggregate results (rows 0, 2, 3). Differences are <0.01 in last decimal place for sum_disc_price and sum_charge.
critical_fixes.fix: Increase output precision or use consistent accumulation order. Current issue likely due to floating-point arithmetic order differences (compiler optimization may reorder additions).
critical_fixes.description:
Row 0: expected sum_disc_price=537759104278.07, actual=537759104278.06 (diff=-0.01)
Row 2: expected sum_base_price=1114302286901.88, actual=1114302286901.87 (diff=-0.01)
Row 3: expected sum_charge=559634780885.09, actual=559634780885.08 (diff=-0.01)
These are typical floating-point rounding errors. Can be fixed by:
- Using Kahan summation for better accuracy
- Ensuring consistent operator evaluation order
- Or accepting small ULP differences as normal for aggregation queries
This is NOT a critical issue—aggregate precision to 0.01 is acceptable for TPC-H.

performance_optimizations.description: Replace CSV parsing (lines 73-91) with mmap-based access to binary column files. Load gendb storage metadata, use mmap() for zero-copy reads of l_shipdate, l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus. No parsing overhead—just pointer arithmetic to access elements. Use madvise(MADV_SEQUENTIAL) for prefetching.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 100-300× speedup. Text parsing of 60M rows → instant mmap. Estimated 14,799 ms → 50-150 ms.

performance_optimizations.technique: Array-Based Aggregation Instead of Hash Table
performance_optimizations.description: Q1 groups by (returnflag, linestatus)—only 6 possible combinations (3 returnflags × 2 linestatuses). Replace std::map (lines 82-154) with fixed-size array of 6 AggregateGroup structs. Index = returnflag_code * 2 + linestatus_code. Eliminates hash table overhead, branch misprediction, and malloc. Direct array indexing is O(1) cache-friendly.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-10× faster aggregation phase. Hash table lookups + insertions → direct array indexing. Estimated improvement from 50-150 ms (after fix 1) → 5-30 ms.

performance_optimizations.technique: Thread-Local Aggregation with Parallel Morsel-Driven Scan
performance_optimizations.description: Use 64 cores for parallel scan. Divide 60M rows into 64 morsels (~1M rows per core). Each thread maintains local 6-element agg array. After scan, merge 64 local arrays into single result. No synchronization during scan—only final O(6×64) merge. Use work-stealing or static partitioning for load balance.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 50-60× speedup on 64-core system (near-linear). Text parsing was serial. With mmap (instant) + parallel agg = ~50 ms → <1 ms per core's share.

performance_optimizations.technique: Zone Map Skipping for Date Filter
performance_optimizations.description: Lineitem is sorted by l_shipdate (per storage_design.json). Zone maps store min/max shipdate per 256K-row block. Query filters l_shipdate <= 1998-09-02. Before processing a block, check zone map: if block_max < cutoff, skip entire block. Saves reading blocks with only future-dated records.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 5-15% improvement. Q1 filter selectivity is 98.4% (per workload_analysis.json)—most blocks will be read. But zone maps eliminate 1.6% of I/O for blocks entirely outside range. Estimated: 50 ms → 45 ms.

performance_optimizations.description: Load only columns used in query: l_shipdate (filter), l_quantity, l_extendedprice, l_discount, l_tax, l_returnflag, l_linestatus (GROUP BY). Saves mmap I/O for unused columns (l_orderkey, l_partkey, l_suppkey, l_linenumber, l_commitdate, l_receiptdate, l_shipinstruct, l_shipmode, l_comment). Reduces per-query memory footprint.
performance_optimizations.expected_improvement: 10-20% memory bandwidth reduction. Query uses 7 of 16 columns = 56% savings potential. Current storage is already columnar, so only map needed columns. Estimated: 45 ms → 40 ms (marginal after fix 1).

summary: Q1 iteration 0 FAILS: 14,799 ms vs. 48 ms baseline (308× slower). Root cause: SEMANTIC ARCHITECTURE ISSUE. Code reads from hardcoded CSV file path using text parsing (std::getline + stringstream) instead of using GenDB binary columnar storage API with mmap. Fixes required: (1) CRITICAL: Replace CSV parsing with mmap-based columnar access (100-300× improvement). (2) MEDIUM: Array-based aggregation for 6 groups (5-10× improvement). (3) Parallel morsel-driven scan with thread-local aggregation (50-60× speedup on 64 cores). (4) Zone map skipping for date filter (5-15%). (5) Column pruning (10-20% memory bandwidth). Minor correctness issue: floating-point rounding in last decimal place—acceptable for TPC-H. Target after all optimizations: <5 ms (fast-path for 60M rows through mmap + 64-thread parallelism).
